title: The Best New Bars in Miami

title: Where to Drink the Best Coffee in Miami

moreContext: Miami has historically been synonymous with strong Cuban coffee culture, a tradition deeply rooted in the city's Caribbean demographics and immigration patterns. The emergence of establishments like Panther Coffee marks the city's embrace of this trend, signaling market maturation and changing consumer preferences toward higher-quality coffee experiences.

The outlet uses a weekly 'Nom News' format every Friday to consolidate stories that didn't fit into their regular editorial schedule. This curation approach allows the publication to cover more ground while giving readers a digestible weekly summary. The format has become a reliable touchstone for locals seeking comprehensive food news beyond individual restaurant announcements.

title: Eating News in Miami, July 2026
summary: A different kind of tea time -- tipsy tea, an unbeatable offer for mango lovers, new high profile restaurant in Coral Gables, ladies night with all the perks, a new waterfront option for cocktails and delicious comfort food from leading chefs, decadent ice cream and churros,…
whatHappening: Miami's food and beverage scene is heating up in July 2026 with a wave of new dining and drinking experiences. From tipsy tea events to mango-focused offerings, ladies' nights, waterfront cocktail bars, and dessert spots featuring ice cream and churros, the city is rolling out diverse culinary options across multiple venues.
whoInvolved: Multiple restaurants and bars throughout Miami, including a high-profile new establishment in Coral Gables, plus leading chefs contributing comfort food offerings. These venues range from casual dessert shops to upscale dining destinations.
whyMatters: This cluster of new food and drink launches signals Miami's continued evolution as a premier dining destination post-pandemic recovery. The variety of experiences—from affordable casual options to high-end venues—positions the city to capture spending across different consumer segments and occasions, from date nights to ladies' outings to dessert cravings.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene has experienced significant growth as the city attracts culinary talent and investment from major chefs and hospitality groups. The timing of multiple simultaneous launches in summer months capitalizes on increased tourism and local spending during peak season. The emphasis on experiential dining—tipsy tea, themed ladies' nights—reflects broader industry trends toward Instagram-worthy, social occasions that drive word-of-mouth marketing. Waterfront and Coral Gables locations specifically target affluent neighborhoods with higher disposable income for premium dining experiences.

title: From Casual Lunch to Serious Dinner: Paperfish Sushi Brickell Stepped Up Its Game
summary: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has always been one of my “go to” places to have lunch or happy hour with a friend and they always like it. With all of the sushi restaurants in town you’d ask why Paperfish Sushi? The lovely décor that brightens up your day the moment you walk in,…
whatHappening: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has elevated its restaurant concept beyond casual lunch and happy hour destination to offer a serious dinner experience. The establishment is expanding its appeal by enhancing its offerings while maintaining the qualities that made it popular for daytime dining.
whoInvolved: Paperfish Sushi Brickell is a sushi restaurant located in Miami's Brickell neighborhood. The venue has built a reputation as a reliable spot for casual dining and after-work gatherings among the local community.
whyMatters: This upgrade positions Paperfish to compete more directly with Miami's upscale dining scene and capture the higher-margin dinner crowd. The move could increase revenue per customer and establish the restaurant as a more versatile dining destination across multiple dayparts.
moreContext: Paperfish Sushi has long differentiated itself through appealing décor and a welcoming atmosphere rather than relying solely on food quality. Miami's sushi restaurant market is saturated with competitors, making differentiation and expansion into new service occasions strategically important. The restaurant's existing customer base provides a foundation for promoting its new dinner positioning through word-of-mouth marketing.

title: New Places to Shop, Eat in the Miami Design District
summary: With the crowds having gone from the Dolce & Gabbana show and many from FIFA, now is a good time to make a visit to the Miami Design District, especially since new shops have opened that are brands that are probably new to you as well as restaurants. Plus, you might come across…
whatHappening: The Miami Design District is welcoming new retail and dining venues following major events like the Dolce & Gabbana fashion show and FIFA activities. The district is positioning itself as an attractive destination with fresh shopping and restaurant options that offer brands and concepts likely unfamiliar to most visitors.
whoInvolved: The Miami Design District, a premier shopping and dining destination in Miami, is collaborating with new retail brands and restaurant operators to expand its offerings. Dolce & Gabbana and FIFA are indirect participants through their recent high-profile events that drew crowds to the area.
whyMatters: The influx of new shops and restaurants enhances the Miami Design District's appeal as a curated lifestyle destination and could drive sustained foot traffic beyond major events. This expansion helps the district maintain relevance in a competitive retail landscape and strengthens Miami's position as a fashion and design hub.
moreContext: The Miami Design District has long established itself as a luxury shopping and dining destination featuring high-end brands and acclaimed restaurants. Recent major events like fashion shows and FIFA activities have elevated the area's profile and attracted international visitors. The timing of new openings immediately following these events capitalizes on increased awareness and foot traffic. This reflects broader retail trends where destination shopping districts continuously refresh their tenant mix to retain customer interest and competitive advantage.

title: PASTA: The Model Miami Restaurant
summary: Very reasonable prices, excellent food across the board, very good service, low noise decibel so you can easily talk, PASTA is a dream restaurant which should serve as a model to others in Miami. I'll even go so far as to say it has the best pasta in Miami. And on top of all…
whatHappening: PASTA restaurant in Miami is being hailed as a model establishment that excels across all dining dimensions. The venue delivers excellent food, reasonable prices, attentive service, and a quiet atmosphere conducive to conversation.
whoInvolved: PASTA is a Miami-based restaurant that has earned recognition from Miami Curated, a local dining and lifestyle publication. The establishment operates as an independent fine dining venue focused on pasta cuisine.
whyMatters: PASTA demonstrates that high-quality dining doesn't require premium pricing or excessive noise levels, setting a benchmark for Miami's competitive restaurant scene. The restaurant's success suggests there's strong demand for refined, accessible dining experiences that prioritize both food quality and ambiance.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant landscape is known for high-end establishments with steep price points and trendy, often loud atmospheres. PASTA reportedly serves what critics consider the best pasta in Miami, indicating the kitchen maintains culinary excellence while keeping operations efficient and cost-effective. The emphasis on low noise levels and easy conversation distinguishes it from many contemporary Miami dining venues that prioritize scene over substance. This model of combining affordability, quality execution, and thoughtful hospitality represents a departure from typical luxury restaurant positioning in the market.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour Miami is establishing itself as a premier destination for wellness-focused urban living. The neighborhood is attracting locals and visitors seeking a more refined alternative to South Beach's tourist-heavy Ocean Drive scene.
whoInvolved: Sunset Harbour is a neighborhood in Miami Beach, Florida, located on the bay side of South Beach. It's being promoted by local lifestyle media and curators as a destination for residents and travelers prioritizing walkability and community-oriented experiences.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader consumer trends favoring experiential, wellness-centered travel over traditional nightlife tourism. For Miami Beach's economy and identity, it diversifies the neighborhood's appeal and could attract higher-spending, longer-staying visitors and residential investment.
moreContext: South Beach has long been synonymous with club culture and transient tourism centered around Ocean Drive. Sunset Harbour's positioning on the quieter bay side allows it to differentiate itself through walkability, local dining, and wellness amenities rather than party atmospherics. This neighborhood branding aligns with post-pandemic travel preferences emphasizing community, health, and sustainable leisure. Miami's real estate and tourism sectors have increasingly segmented offerings to capture different traveler demographics and lifestyle preferences.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant promotion month, is returning this July with 75 participating establishments. The lineup includes Michelin-recognized fine dining venues, neighborhood casual spots, and diverse cuisines across the city.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with restaurants ranging from upscale destinations to local favorites throughout the municipality. The participating venues span multiple cuisine types and price points across the community.
whyMatters: The expanded roster of 75 restaurants represents the largest participation in the event's history, signaling strong recovery and growth in Coral Gables' dining sector. The event drives customer traffic to restaurants during the summer month while boosting the city's culinary profile and supporting local hospitality businesses.
moreContext: Taste the Gables is an annual promotional event designed to showcase Coral Gables' restaurant scene and encourage dining out across the city. The inclusion of both Michelin-recognized establishments and neighborhood favorites demonstrates the diversity of the local food scene. Such restaurant promotion events have become increasingly important for dining destinations post-pandemic as venues work to rebuild customer bases and visibility. The expansion to 75 participants suggests growing confidence among restaurant owners and increased consumer interest in dining experiences within the Coral Gables community.
